name: Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop
name_original: Colada Peña Betoño a Pagogan – circular desde Vitoria
description: Experience the moderate 17.5-mile Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop mountain biking trail with scenic views over the Llanada.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Vitoria-Gasteiz offers diverse terrain, from urban green spaces to surrounding natural parks. The city's Green Belt provides accessible routes, while the Montes de Vitoria mountain range features varied landscapes with peaks like Kapildui. The Llanada Alavesa presents a network of paths across a flatter area, and the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir offers lakeside routes. Best mountain bike trails around Vitoria-Gasteiz The most popular mountain bike trail is Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop, a 17.5 miles (28.1 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views over the Llanada Alavesa landscape. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Montes de Vitoria and Colada de Peña Betoño Loop, a moderate 15.3 miles (24.6 km) path. This route provides a moderate mountain biking experience through gall-oak groves in the Montes de Vitoria. Local mountain bikers also love the Landa Lakeside Path – View of Barracones Reservoir loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz, a 42.9 miles (69.0 km) trail leading around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Mountain biking around Vitoria-Gasteiz is defined by urban green spaces, the Montes de Vitoria mountain range, and the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. The network offers options for easy, family-friendly routes as well as more challenging trails for experienced riders. The routes in Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. More than 5000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Vitoria-Gasteiz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: There are over 230 mountain bike trails around Vitoria-Gasteiz, offering a wide range of options for all skill levels. These include routes through urban green spaces, the Montes de Vitoria mountain range, and around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir.

Q: What types of mountain bike trails can I find in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz offers a diverse range of mountain biking experiences. You'll find easy, family-friendly routes within the city's Green Belt, moderate trails through gall-oak groves in the Montes de Vitoria, and more challenging paths with significant elevation gains in the surrounding natural parks. There are 18 easy, 123 moderate, and 95 difficult trails available.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz is well-suited for families and beginners. The city's Green Belt provides numerous accessible and safe paths suitable for all ages and skill levels, often connecting to other routes like the Vasco Navarro greenway. The region offers 18 easy trails perfect for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, the Montes de Vitoria range offers trails with varied terrain and significant elevation gains. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24693607" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Saint Marina Hermitage – Ganalto Summit Cross loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 30.7-mile (49.4 km) route with over 550 meters of ascent. The natural parks surrounding the city also provide more demanding activities.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Vitoria-Gasteiz are circular. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056083195/colada-de-pena-betono-and-pagogan-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 17.5-mile (28.1 km) circular trail offering scenic views over the Llanada Alavesa. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1052531695/montes-de-vitoria-and-colada-de-pena-betono-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Vitoria and Colada de Peña Betoño Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 15.3-mile (24.6 km) circular path through gall-oak groves.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural attractions can I see along the trails?
A: The region around Vitoria-Gasteiz is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3261901" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landa Lakeside Path&lt;/a&gt; around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir, or discover mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3076159" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zaldiaran Pass&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5461169" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vitoria Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features diverse landscapes including gall-oak groves, beech forests, and kermes-oak forests, and is home to wildlife.

Q: Can I access mountain bike trails using public transport?
A: Vitoria-Gasteiz is known as the 'city of the bike' with 171 kilometers of bike lanes. Many routes are accessible directly from the city center, and a green route from the city's cycling lanes allows access to the provincial parks of Garaio and Landa around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir. This makes it convenient to reach trailheads by cycling from public transport hubs within the city.

Q: Are there any trails around the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Ullibarri-Gamboa Reservoir is a popular area for cycling. A green route from the city's cycling lanes provides access to the provincial parks of Garaio and Landa, allowing cyclists to ride around the reservoir. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40354597" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Landa Lakeside Path – View of Barracones Reservoir loop from Vitoria-Gasteiz&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.9-mile (69.0 km) trail that circles the reservoir.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: The mountain bike trails around Vitoria-Gasteiz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 600 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the accessibility of routes from the city, and the beautiful natural landscapes, especially the Green Belt and the Montes de Vitoria.

Q: Is Vitoria-Gasteiz suitable for gravel biking?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz is also supportive of gravel biking. The region's network of trails and low-traffic roads makes it an excellent destination for this growing modality, enhancing cyclotourism safety and offering diverse routes for gravel enthusiasts.

Q: Are there bike rental options available in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: Yes, Vitoria-Gasteiz offers several options for bike rentals. Public electric bike rentals are available through MugiBIKE, and numerous companies in the city provide rentals for bikes, scooters, and tricycles, making it easy to explore the area even if you don't bring your own bike.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Vitoria-Gasteiz?
A: While specific seasonal conditions can vary, the diverse terrain of Vitoria-Gasteiz allows for mountain biking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery. However, trails in the Montes de Vitoria and natural parks might be more challenging in winter due to weather conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of mountain biking experience is required for this trail?
faqs.answer: The Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop is classified as a moderate mountain bike ride. It demands good physical fitness and advanced riding skills, corresponding to a Single Track Scale (STS) rating of S2. This means you should expect some technical sections along the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, featuring an irregular surface with stones, gravel tracks, and ascending sections. You'll encounter singletrack parts, many of which are shaded. Historically, it was an old cart path, so you might still find remnants of cobblestone sections.

faqs.question: What natural features and viewpoints can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect a rich natural environment with beautiful views over the Llanada (plain). The trail winds through oak groves and offers glimpses of pine plantations. Notable highlights include the GR25 Scenic Section and the Pagogan summit.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop?
faqs.answer: Since the trail is a loop that conveniently starts and finishes from Vitoria, you can typically find parking within the city or at designated access points on the outskirts. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but starting from Vitoria provides flexibility.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: During autumn, the scenery is particularly striking with vibrant, changing leaves, making it an excellent time to visit. However, the trail offers a rich natural environment year-round.

faqs.question: Is the Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the trail passes through natural areas, specific regulations regarding dogs are not provided. It's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all users.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for riding the Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop itself, as it's a public trail. However, it's always good practice to check local regulations before your visit.

faqs.question: What are the regulations for mountain biking in Montes de Vitoria?
faqs.answer: As the trail connects the urban area with surrounding forests in the Álava region, which includes parts of Montes de Vitoria, general regulations for outdoor activities apply. This typically includes staying on marked trails, respecting private property, and practicing Leave No Trace principles. Always check local signage for specific rules regarding mountain biking in protected areas.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other trails, including the Colada de Peña Betoño, Lautadako Bira, Artzaintza naturbidea, and the Vuelta al Anillo Verde/Eraztun Berdearen bira. You'll also briefly cross parts of the Vasco-Navarroaren bide berdea.

faqs.question: What is the historical significance of the Colada de Peña Betoño?
faqs.answer: The Colada de Peña Betoño is historically significant as an ancient commercial route that linked La Rioja Alavesa with Vitoria. It was used for transporting goods like livestock, wine, and agricultural products. Today, it has been revitalized as a green corridor, connecting the city with its forested surroundings.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike loop?
faqs.answer: The Colada de Peña Betoño and Pagogan Loop typically takes around 2 hours and 30 minutes to complete by mountain bike, covering a distance of approximately 28 kilometers.
